title: The Intelligent Investor
author: Benjamin Graham
core_idea: Intelligent investing requires emotional discipline, rational analysis, and a value-oriented philosophy. Graham distinguishes between investing and speculation, introducing timeless ideas like 'margin of safety' and 'Mr. Market'.
key_concepts: Investment is based on analysis, safety of principal, and adequate return.
The market is your servant, not your guide (Mr. Market).
Buy below intrinsic value to ensure a margin of safety.
Emotions are the investor’s greatest enemy.
Differentiate between defensive (passive) and enterprising (active) investors.
principles: Maintain a 50/50 portfolio split between stocks and bonds.
Invest in large, financially sound companies with stable dividends.
Always analyze fundamentals and ignore market hype.
If you lack time or expertise, use low-cost index funds.
notable_quote: The essence of investment management is the management of risks, not the management of returns.
practical_lessons: Be patient — time is your greatest ally.
Buy with a margin of safety.
Keep emotions out of investment decisions.
